lab_description: We focus on the interdisciplinary research of Quantum Artificial Intelligence (QAI), with the core theme of the bidirectional empowerment between quantum computing and artificial intelligence, anchored in the 'Quantum for AI' paradigm while covering 'AI for Quantum' and quantum-inspired machine learning. Our primary research areas and core methods include: 1) Quantum machine learning algorithm design, including parametrized quantum circuits (PQC), quantum neural networks (QNN), quantum convolutional neural networks (QCNN), quantum graph neural networks (QGNN), quantum generative models, quantum recurrent neural networks, quantum reinforcement learning, variational quantum algorithms (VQAs), and quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA); 2) Automated design and compilation optimization of quantum circuits, including differentiable quantum architecture search, quantum job scheduling, and quantum circuit synthesis & optimization for NISQ devices; 3) AI-assisted quantum system optimization, including quantum state property estimation, learning-based quantum error mitigation, quantum noise suppression, and semi-supervised/self-supervised learning for quantum data; 4) Quantum-inspired machine learning, including high-dimensional graph embedding, network representation learning, and efficient representation learning in quantum Hilbert space. Our key research interests and application scenarios cover numerical solution of partial differential equations (PDE) via quantum neural operators, and the standardization system construction & industrial implementation of quantum artificial intelligence. We prioritize papers that propose novel quantum machine learning models, advance the practicality of quantum algorithms on NISQ devices, explore industrial-grade applications of QAI, develop AI methods for quantum system optimization, or advance the standardization of quantum artificial intelligence.
